evals.prompt: I need to add live notifications to my web app. Should I use WebSockets or Server-Sent Events (SSE)? Only the server sends data to the client after initial connection.
evals.expected_output: Comparison and recommendation favoring SSE for unidirectional data.
expectations: Identifies that SSE is better suited for unidirectional (server-to-client) data flow
Mentions trade-offs (e.g., SSE has a connection limit in HTTP/1.1)
Provides a high-level architectural pattern for the solution

evals.prompt: Provide a basic Node.js Express implementation pattern for Server-Sent Events (SSE).
evals.expected_output: Code example demonstrating SSE endpoint in Express.
expectations: Includes correct HTTP headers (Content-Type: text/event-stream, Cache-Control: no-cache, Connection: keep-alive)
Shows how to write data to the stream (res.write('data: ...\n\n'))
Demonstrates a simple loop or event listener sending data
